data.title: China to Boost Credit Support for Private Enterprises
data.description:
China's financial regulator pledged to expand credit support for private enterprises, emphasizing the need for stable credit growth.

The National Financial Regulatory Administration said it is also prioritizing first-time loans, renewals and credit for small businesses.

Banks and insurers will be guided to improve financial services, ensuring fair market access for businesses of all ownership types, the regulator added.

data.title: Huadong Medicine Unit Gets China Nod to Market Diabetes Drug; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
China's National Medical Products Administration accepted Huadong Medicine's marketing application for its degludec insulin injection, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The drug is indicated to treat adult type 2 diabetes.

The pharmaceutical company has invested 122.9 million yuan in the research and development of the drug, the filing said.

The company's shares rose more than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: SingLand, UOL, Kheng Leong JV Secures Chinese Development Project with 8.9 Billion Yuan Bid
data.description:
Singapore Land's (SGX: U06) indirect joint venture company, Qin Rui Jia Realty, and China Jinmao Holdings were awarded a tender to acquire and develop a government land in Hong Kou, Shanghai, for a total consideration of about 8.96 billion yuan.

Qin Rui Jia Realty is a joint venture between SingLand subsidiary SingLand China Holdings, UOL unit UOL Capital Investments and Kheng Leong subsidiary Peak Star, with the companies holding respective stakes of 30%, 40% and 30% in the JV, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The land parcel will be converted into a residential project. Following the award of the tender, Qin Rui Jia and China Jinmao will enter into a joint venture agreement to form a JV company on a 10:90 basis. The JV will complete the purchase and development of the site.

The land has a total area of approximately 19,319 square meters with a 70 year leasehold.

data.title: CATL, Volkswagen to Collaborate on EV Batteries Amid Intensifying Competition
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Chinese battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology will collaborate with Volkswagen on electric vehicle batteries, new materials and other auto parts as the automaker seeks to adapt to the fast-evolving and competitive EV market.

The Chinese company, in a statement released on Friday, announced that it has signed a memorandum of understanding with Volkswagen to strengthen its partnership in EV battery research and development, new material applications and the development of auto parts.

Volkswagen stated that partnering with CATL will help provide better battery solutions for its customers, support its supply chain and better meet the growing needs of the EV market.

The move comes amid intensifying competition in the Chinese EV market, where multiple local automakers are launching cheaper, more advanced EVs at a much faster pace than foreign brands.

Volkswagen has also teamed up with Chinese EV maker XPeng to build over 20,000 charging stations across 420 cities in China.

CATL accounted for more than one-third of the global battery market in 2024, more than twice the volume of BYD, which ranked second, according to Seoul-based SNE Research.

CATL's Shenzhen-listed shares rose 0.7% to 272.02 yuan, or equivalent of $37.49.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Multistack International Settles Legal Dispute with Danfoss, Secures Nearly $2 Million Payout
data.description:
Multistack International has reached a settlement in its legal dispute with Danish company Danfoss, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Under the settlement, Danfoss will make an immediate cash payment to the company of $1.9 million and will provide a one-off credit of $100,000 to be used for the purchase of goods from Danfoss, the filing added.

Danfoss has appointed the company to provide product support and on-site repairs and service for Danfoss products for five years, among other agreements under the settlement, per the filing.

The settlement is expected to be completed around March 7.

data.title: HKEX Likely to Remain Supported by Rise of AI Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is likely to remain supported by the rise of AI, which has fueled investor sentiment towards tech stocks, supporting a surge in turnover volume, according to Citi Research analyst Michael Zhang in a note. This support should persist in the near term, and HKEX is a key beneficiary of the recovery in market sentiment toward Chinese stocks, Zhang says. The increasing number of dual-listings of mainland companies in Hong Kong could also support the recovery of the Hong Kong IPO market, Zhang adds. Citi upgrades its rating on HKEX to buy from sell, raising the target price to HK$370 from HK$275. Shares are up 5.4% at HK$345.20. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0340 GMT - Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is likely to remain supported by the rise of AI, which has fueled investor sentiment towards tech stocks, supporting a surge in turnover volume, according to Citi Research analyst Michael Zhang in a note. This support should persist in the near term, and HKEX is a key beneficiary of the recovery in market sentiment toward Chinese stocks, Zhang says. The increasing number of dual-listings of mainland companies in Hong Kong could also support the recovery of the Hong Kong IPO market, Zhang adds. Citi upgrades its rating on HKEX to buy from sell, raising the target price to HK$370 from HK$275. Shares are up 5.4% at HK$345.20. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0335 GMT - Genting Singapore's cost pressures could ease in 2026, with earnings supported by new attractions, luxury rooms and retail stores, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. The opening of its new all-suite hotel in Singapore has been delayed to 3Q, while its S.E.A. Aquarium will close for 2.5 months in 1H, ahead of the Singapore Oceanarium relaunch in July, they write. New retail stores will begin operating in the coming months, they say. Higher initial costs to prepare for these openings could soften 1H earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization margins, they say. "However, we expect a sequential recovery as income from the new openings starts to flow in," they add. DBS maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$0.95. Shares are at S$0.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0332 GMT - Kossan Rubber Industries' earnings are expected to weaken sequentially in 1Q before improving in later quarters, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. Chee cuts the glove maker's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 49% and 31%, respectively, given its 4Q 2024 profit shortfall. The glove maker could see 8.5%-10.5% weaker sales volume for its glove and clean-room segment in 1Q as most front-loading activities by U.S. customers has already occurred in 4Q of 2024, he says. The global medical glove demand-supply balance is now projected to stabilize in 2026 instead of 2025, Chee adds. Hong Leong cuts the stock's target price to MYR2.81 from MYR3.00 but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR1.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0329 GMT - Foreign fund inflows into Chinese equities have picked up over the past two weeks as DeepSeek draws investors' attention to the market, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. The Chinese startup has shown that the country has the ability to compete at the highest level in the artificial-intelligence space, they add. The valuation gap between China and emerging markets has been narrowing to 12.5% from 20.3%, they note. DeepSeek has also triggered a rotation in the Chinese market from upstream hardware to midstream software and services as well as downstream applications, the analysts say. They note that 120 A-share companies have integrated DeepSeek into their products or services. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0323 GMT - Advanced Info Service's earnings may benefit from Thailand's likely economic growth, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. This should lead to increases in the Thai mobile phone operator's prepaid top-ups such as for higher data consumption, the analyst says. The brokerage now forecasts the company's 2025 core profit to grow 12%, thanks to drivers including spectrum cost savings and falling interest expenses. The company's fixed broadband revenue should also outperform its mobile revenue due to growth in its fixed broadband subscribers. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to THB312.00 from THB307.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.35% lower at THB282.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - NetEase's fundamentals remain intact despite its lower-than-expected 4Q mobile game revenue, HSBC analysts say in a research note. NetEase's strong deferred revenue, which implies a solid performance of legacy PC games and a successful launch of "Marvel Rivals," could outweigh weakness in its mobile games and support game growth in 2025, they say. HSBC lowers its 2025 revenue forecast slightly as NetEase focuses on lifting the profitability of its nongame segments, which could weigh on topline growth in coming quarters. However, it raises its 2025-2026 profit estimate by 3% due to the game maker's expected better cost-efficiency. HSBC raises NetEase's ADR target to $120.00 from $117.00 and keeps a buy call. Shares last ended at $101.42.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0257 GMT - Nexon's 2025-2026 earnings outlook appears positive, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report as they maintain the stock's buy rating. The Japan-listed game developer has a solid game pipeline targeting different game genres and addressable markets as well as strong cash-generating franchise intellectual properties, the analysts note. Nexon could deliver relatively decent, sustainable growth in revenue and operating profit this year, thanks to drivers including new game launches in March. However, Daiwa cuts its 2025-2026 EPS forecasts for Nexon by 6.8%-7.5% to partly reflect the management's revised cost strategy for staff and marketing. It lowers the stock's target price to Y3,000 from Y3,300. Shares are 0.4% higher at Y2,029. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - Thai Union Group may gain from its business transformation in 2025, UOB Kay Hian's Kampon Akaravarinchai says in a research report as the brokerage maintains a buy rating on the stock. The company expects US$15 million costs savings and US$17 million operating-profit increase for subsidiary i-Tail Corp., the analyst notes. It also said that some of these benefits will be used to reinvest in transformation projects and in developing skills for employees, the analyst adds. The brokerage trims the stock's target price to THB15.00 from THB15.50, based on estimated five-year price-to-earnings mean of 13X. Shares closed 2.5% lower at THB11.70 on Thursday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - While AI and data center demand offer long-term potential for Inari Amertron, geopolitical risks and trade tensions could weigh on growth, Kenanga IB analyst Cheow Ming Liang writes in a note. The World Semiconductor Trade Statistics expects the global semiconductor market to grow 11.2% in 2025, but uncertainties remain amid rising trade tensions, which could impact the industry's short-term outlook, he notes. Cheow revises Inari's earnings estimates for FY2025 and FY2026 lower by 21% and 23%, respectively, as the company's fiscal 1H earnings fell short of expectations. Kenanga downgrades Inari's rating to market perform from outperform, while cutting the target price to MYR2.80 from MYR3.27. Shares are 3.0% lower at MYR2.57.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0228 GMT - Genting Singapore's earnings outlook will likely be generally stable, Maybank IB's Yin Shao Yang says in a note. The 'Minion Land' attraction opened in February, while a super luxury all-suite hotel and the Singapore Oceanarium are expected to open in 3Q, the analyst says. Parts of the Forum complex will also open over the course of the year, Yin adds. The integrated resorts operator expects 1H margins to be suppressed due to preopening expenses, but 2H margins could normalize, Yin notes. In the long term, Genting Singapore expects the attractions to be Ebitda accretive. Maybank maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.01. Shares are at S$0.76. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0217 GMT - CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Singtel's new FY 2025 earnings guidance looks encouraging, the OCBC Investment Research team says in note. The telecom conglomerate has raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth forecast to high teens-to-low twenties from prior forecast of low double digits, the team says. Singtel is likely a key beneficiary of Singapore's drive for technology and innovation as highlighted in recent Singapore Budget, the team says. The government will allocate S$150 million for a new "Enterprise Compute Initiative," which Singtel could tap to expand its enterprise services and cloud-computing offerings. OCBC raises stock's fair value estimate to S$4.00 from S$3.85 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 2.1% lower at S$3.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Santos Target Price Raised 0.6% to A$8.15/Share by UBS>STO.AU
Malaysian Pacific's Profit Rises in Fiscal Q2
data.description:
Malaysian Pacific Industries' attributable profit rose to 40 million ringgit in the fiscal second quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 32.2 million ringgit a year earlier.

Earnings per share went up to 0.200 ringgit from 0.1617 ringgit a year ago, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Revenue edged up year over year to 530.5 million ringgit from 522.8 million ringgit, the integrated circuit manufacturer said.

data.title: Mayur Resources Completes Bookbuild for AU$100.1 Million CDI Placement; Shares Fall Past 11%
data.description:
Mayur Resource completed the bookbuild for its around AU$100.1 million institutional placement of around 357.5 million new Chess depository interests (CDI) at an offer price of AU$0.28 per CDI, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm will issue around 106.5 million CDIs, raising around AU$29.8 million in the first tranche.

It also proposes to issue around 251 million new CDIs to raise around AU$70.3 million, subject to shareholder approval.

The proceeds will fund the development of the first and second stages of the company's Central Lime project in Papua New Guinea.

The firm's shares fell past 11% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Kossan Rubber Could See Sequentially Weaker 1Q Earnings — Market Talk
data.description: Kossan Rubber Industries' earnings are expected to weaken sequentially in 1Q before improving in later quarters, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. Chee cuts the glove maker's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 49% and 31%, respectively, given its 4Q 2024 profit shortfall. The glove maker could see 8.5%-10.5% weaker sales volume for its glove and clean-room segment in 1Q as most front-loading activities by U.S. customers has already occurred in 4Q of 2024, he says. The global medical glove demand-supply balance is now projected to stabilize in 2026 instead of 2025, Chee adds. Hong Leong cuts the stock's target price to MYR2.81 from MYR3.00 but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR1.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Genetic Technologies Creditors Set for Second Reconvened Meeting on Feb. 27
data.description:
The voluntary administrators of Genetic Technologies said the company's creditors will have their second reconvened meeting on Feb. 27, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Creditors will vote on whether the company should execute a Deed of Company Arrangement; to end the administration process; or to wind up the company, the filing said.

data.title: Solaris Energy Infrastructure Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Solaris Energy Infrastructure Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 12 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 15 cents. The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 13 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 10 cents to 14 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 52% to $96.30 million from a year ago; analysts expected $94.30 million.

  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 19 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $6.25 million.

  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure Inc shares had risen by 4.3% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 17.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the oil related services and equipment peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Solaris Energy Infrastructure Inc is $37.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 21 at 03:32 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Newmont Alert to Cost Risks From Tariff-Fueled Currency Swings
data.description:
By Rhiannon Hoyle

Newmont is alive to the risk of rising costs at its global operations if proposed U.S. tariffs stoke volatility on currency markets, but doesn't anticipate an upheaval in sales given it mostly ships gold to buyers elsewhere.

Denver-based Newmont, the world's biggest gold producer, runs mines in places including Mexico and Canada, who President Trump has threatened with 25% tariffs as part of an aggressive trade agenda that has jolted global markets.

"In terms of our business, it's something that is live, that we're constantly monitoring, and clearly what we're seeing is that it's shifting on almost a daily basis," Chief Executive Tom Palmer said following Newmont's 2024 fiscal results.

"We're monitoring for foreign-exchange volatility, given the places we operate in around the world and the impact that may or may not have on our cost base, both positively and negatively," he said.

Uncertainty over tariffs, which can affect currencies in several ways, has already caused market gyrations and currency traders have been bracing for more whiplash ahead.

For U.S. firms that do business abroad, big currency swings can make it costlier or cheaper to pay for labor and materials. Newmont has operations in nine countries, which helps mitigate some of the risk, Palmer said.

Newmont is also monitoring how trade negotiations might impact equipment and other products it buys from the U.S. to run its mines elsewhere, Palmer said.

The company reported a 5% rise year over year in its all-in sustaining cost, a key measure of how much it costs to keep a mine running. It forecast a further rise in 2025.

Palmer said productivity and cost improvements will be among Newmont's top priorities for the year ahead. "Following a period of integration and rationalization, it's clear to us that there are still areas in our business that can be more efficient," he said.

Newmont has been reshaping its business over the past 15 months following its $17.5 billion acquisition of Australia's Newcrest Mining, the gold-mining industry's biggest-ever takeover. Newmont is narrowing its focus on running large, low-cost mines with long lives in low-risk places to operate, Palmer said.

It has recently struck deals to sell a number of operations it deemed non-core, including the Cripple Creek & Victor mine in its home state of Colorado.

Newmont, which said it aimed to get at least $2 billion from selling mines within two years of buying Newcrest, now expects to make up to $4.3 billion from the sale of noncore assets and other investments, Palmer said.

Newmont's global operations include the giant Peñasquito mine in Mexico's Zacatecas state and Brucejack operation in British Columbia.

Palmer said he doesn't expect sales from those operations to be significantly affected if the U.S. goes ahead with tariffs on its neighbors, as they ship only small volumes of gold doré bars to U.S. refineries. Trump on Feb. 3 delayed proposed tariffs on Mexico and Canada for a month to negotiate with those countries' leaders.

"A lot of that product, whether it be doré bar or concentrate, heads out into international markets," Palmer said, without specifying how much. Doré bars are made mostly of gold, but also contain silver and other metals.

Newmont's higher operating costs last year were more than offset by a jump in both gold production and prices. The company said its adjusted net income, a key measure of profitability that strips out some one-off charges, tripled in 2024 versus the year prior.

Gold prices have been notching record highs, posting their biggest annual gain since 2010 last year. Trump's tariff threats have helped fan the rally in prices, which has also been fueled by wars in the Middle East and Ukraine, an uncertain rates outlook and strong central bank demand.

Palmer said higher gold prices won't change Newmont's approach to investment or shareholder returns, and that it will remain focused on margin over volume.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: Australian Dollar Reaches 10-Week High
data.description:
The Australian dollar rose to around $0.64 on Friday, reaching its highest level in ten weeks as strong economic data fueled expectations of a more gradual easing cycle from the Reserve Bank of Australia.

Data showed that private sector growth in Australia extended into a fifth consecutive month in February, with both manufacturing and services activities remaining in expansionary territory.

Additionally, recent reports indicated that employment rose more than expected in December, pointing to a robust labor market.

Earlier this week, the RBA lowered its cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.1%, as anticipated.

However, the central bank took a more cautious stance on further rate cuts, citing concerns that the disinflation process could stall.

In a press conference following the meeting, RBA Governor Michele Bullock emphasized that the central bank cannot yet declare victory over inflation and cautioned that there may be limited room for additional cuts.

data.description: Genting Singapore's cost pressures could ease in 2026, with earnings supported by new attractions, luxury rooms and retail stores, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. data.title: Australia's Alcidion rises on winning $24 mln contract
data.description:
** Shares of Alcidion Group rise 29.4% to A$0.11, hitting their highest level since mid-October 2023

** Healthcare tech firm wins contract valued at A$37.5 mln ($24 mln) with UK's North Cumbria Integrated Care NHS Foundation Trust

** Contract for use of co's Miya Precision platform to provide an electronic patient record

** 17.9 mln shares traded, highest since early-Nov 2020

** Stock up 83.3% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.5623 Australian dollars)

data.title: AustralianSuper to pay $17 million over failure to merge duplicate accounts
data.description:
AustralianSuper, the country's largest pension fund, was fined A$27 million ($17.28 million) after a court found it had failed to identify and merge duplicate member accounts, the corporate watchdog said on Friday.

The firm, which has over 3.5 million members and A$365 billion in assets, did not have adequate procedures in place to the merge the identical accounts of around 90,700 members between July 2013 and March 2023, the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) said.

As a result, the affected members incurred about A$69 million in losses as they paid multiple sets of administration fees and insurance premiums, ASIC said.

In December 2021, AustralianSuper reported a potential failure to comply with the ASIC's obligations to consolidate duplicate accounts, and in September 2023, the regulator initiated a lawsuit against the pension fund.

($1 = 1.5627 Australian dollars)

data.title: Stocks in Hong Kong Hit 35-month High
data.description:
HK50 increased to a 35-month high of 23243.00 Index Points.

Over the past 4 weeks, Hong Kong Stock Market Index (HK50) gained 17.41%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 38.7%.

data.title: Transurban Target Price Raised 0.7% to A$14.85/Share by UBS>TCL.AU
Magellan Target Price Cut 14% to A$8.85/Share by UBS>MFG.AU
Abrdn to launch China asset management venture with Citic unit, sources say
data.description:
Abrdn is in advanced talks with Citic Bank to launch an asset management joint venture in China, two people with knowledge of the matter said, as the British firm looks to bolster its presence in the world's second-largest economy.

The discussions follow a warming in British-Sino relations and contrast with decisions by several Western financial institutions in recent years to either cut China jobs or shelve their expansion plans on concerns about the health of the Chinese economy and tensions between Beijing and Washington.

Abrdn, the UK's second-largest independent asset manager, expects to hold a majority stake in the venture with the rest owned by Citic Bank's unit Citic Wealth, said the people who declined to be identified as the talks are confidential.

Citic Wealth is China's third-largest bank-owned wealth management unit with 2 trillion yuan ($275 billion) in assets as of the end of last of year.

Abrdn declined to comment. Citic Bank, the main banking arm of China's central government-owned conglomerate Citic Group, did not respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Abrdn and Citic Bank have been in talks for the last couple of years about either setting up a venture in mainland China or abrdn taking a stake in Citic Wealth, said the sources.

The discussions gained fresh impetus after the revival of high-level economic and financial talks between China and the UK last month after having been frozen for nearly six years, they added.

($1 = 7.2724 Chinese yuan )

data.title: MA Financial Target Price Raised 28% to A$9.20/Share by UBS>MAF.AU
IPH Target Price Cut 14% to A$6.80/Share by UBS>IPH.AU
Foreign Funds Returning to China's Equity Market — Market Talk
data.description: Foreign fund inflows into Chinese equities have picked up over the past two weeks as DeepSeek draws investors' attention to the market, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. The Chinese startup has shown that the country has the ability to compete at the highest level in the artificial-intelligence space, they add. The valuation gap between China and emerging markets has been narrowing to 12.5% from 20.3%, they note. DeepSeek has also triggered a rotation in the Chinese market from upstream hardware to midstream software and services as well as downstream applications, the analysts say. data.title: Goodman Target Price Cut 4.3% to A$36.80/Share by UBS>GMG.AU
Senetas to Sell Votiro Stake to Menlo Security
data.description:
Senetas said its unit Votiro Group's board has approved entry into a definitive share purchase agreement to sell the unit to Menlo Security, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company currently holds 55% of Votiro and the transaction proceeds to be received by the unit will be in the form of 40% cash and 60% as shares in Menlo, the filing added.

The base consideration for the deal is around AU$60 million along with the potential for incremental consideration, subject to conditions, per the filing.

About 45% of the base consideration is to be deferred for a year following completion of the transaction and its payment remains conditional on the renewal of key customer contracts.

data.title: Telstra Target Price Raised 2.3% to A$4.50/Share by UBS>TLS.AU
Kossan Rubber Industries Target Price Cut to MYR2.81 From MYR3.00 by Hong Leong IB >7153.KU

(ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - While AI and data center demand offer long-term potential for Inari Amertron, geopolitical risks and trade tensions could weigh on growth, Kenanga IB analyst Cheow Ming Liang writes in a note. The World Semiconductor Trade Statistics expects the global semiconductor market to grow 11.2% in 2025, but uncertainties remain amid rising trade tensions, which could impact the industry's short-term outlook, he notes. Cheow revises Inari's earnings estimates for FY2025 and FY2026 lower by 21% and 23%, respectively, as the company's fiscal 1H earnings fell short of expectations. Kenanga downgrades Inari's rating to market perform from outperform, while cutting the target price to MYR2.80 from MYR3.27. Shares are 3.0% lower at MYR2.57.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0228 GMT - Genting Singapore's earnings outlook will likely be generally stable, Maybank IB's Yin Shao Yang says in a note. The 'Minion Land' attraction opened in February, while a super luxury all-suite hotel and the Singapore Oceanarium are expected to open in 3Q, the analyst says. Parts of the Forum complex will also open over the course of the year, Yin adds. The integrated resorts operator expects 1H margins to be suppressed due to preopening expenses, but 2H margins could normalize, Yin notes. In the long term, Genting Singapore expects the attractions to be Ebitda accretive. Maybank maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.01. Shares are at S$0.76. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0217 GMT - CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Singtel's new FY 2025 earnings guidance looks encouraging, the OCBC Investment Research team says in note. The telecom conglomerate has raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth forecast to high teens-to-low twenties from prior forecast of low double digits, the team says. Singtel is likely a key beneficiary of Singapore's drive for technology and innovation as highlighted in recent Singapore Budget, the team says. The government will allocate S$150 million for a new "Enterprise Compute Initiative," which Singtel could tap to expand its enterprise services and cloud-computing offerings. OCBC raises stock's fair value estimate to S$4.00 from S$3.85 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 2.1% lower at S$3.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0146 GMT - Genting Singapore's earnings growth could be supported by its new phase of expansion at Resorts World Sentosa, say Citi Research analysts in a note. RWS is expected to see significant improvement in its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization from mid-2025 onwards, they write. There are new non-gaming amenities opening in July. The recently opened "Minion Land" is also getting very good traction and positive feedback from visitors, they add. Citi maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.14. Shares are at S$0.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0142 GMT - Petronas Dagangan may face risks to long-term demand from a potential expansion of Malaysia's RON95 fuel subsidy reforms, as well as from decarbonization efforts and the electric-vehicle transition trend, TA Securities analyst Hafriz Hezry says in a note. The impending subsidy cut on its own is expected to affect only the top 15% of earners in Malaysia, with likely minimal immediate impact on the Malaysian downstream petroleum product retailer's sales volume as these consumers are unlikely to significantly reduce consumption, he says. TA Securities downgrades Petronas Dagangan to sell from hold following share-price gains over the past three months. It maintains a MYR18.20 target price. Shares are 5.5% higher at MYR20.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0141 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rises 1.8% to 22976.08 in early trade, led by tech stocks. Market sentiment is likely boosted by Alibaba's earnings which showed strong quarterly profits driven by growth in its cloud unit and e-commerce, the Saxo APAC research team writes in a note. Among advancers, Alibaba Group shares rise 8.7%, Tencent gains 3.3% and JD.com is 3.0% higher. The Hang Seng Tech Index adds 2.7%. Meanwhile, Henderson Land Development falls 1.9%, Sands China loses 1.7% and CK Asset sheds 1.3%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0141 GMT - Chinese shares are higher in early trade amid positive sentiment boosted by Alibaba's earnings. Investors are positive about long-term prospects of Chinese tech stocks for their applications with artificial intelligence. Semiconductor and tech stocks are leading the gains with Cambricon Technologies up 4.3%, Huaqin Technology rising 4.9% and Hundsun Technologies adding 2.7%. Chinese auto stocks and home appliance stocks are leading the losses with Seres Group and SAIC Motor each down 1.2%. Haier Smart Home is off 0.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index rises 0.1% to 3355.20, the Shenzhen Composite Index gains 0.4% and the ChiNext Price Index is up 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0128 GMT - Beacon Lighting is nearing an inflection point in the rate of earnings growth, says Morgans, as momentum in its trade business continues and lower interest rates give retail sales a shot in the arm. Analyst Emily Porter says Beacon Lighting has around 5% of a A$2 billion trade market for lighting and ceiling fans in Australia. "Building up share from this low base can more than offset the effect of a subdued overall market," says Morgans, which has an add call on the stock. The bank also points to signs that the retail cycle is beginning to turn. "We expect this to gather force as the recent interest rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia stimulates a recovery in consumer confidence," Morgans says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
